checks: Caption-VO mismatch — scan word by word from the FIRST caption to the LAST. Compare every caption line against the transcript. Flag any line where even one word differs: wrong word, missing word, extra word, wrong tense, wrong spelling. Do NOT stop at the first mismatch — scan the entire video equally. SPECIALIZED VOCAB EXCEPTION: for botanical names, ingredient names, brand names, medical terms, and product names, Whisper frequently mishears. Do NOT flag as a mismatch based on Whisper alone — only flag if the caption text itself is visually wrong (e.g., caption says 'Ajatica' when you can confirm the correct spelling is 'Asiatica'). Trust the caption for specialized terms unless the caption itself is clearly misspelled.

Duplicate captions — two separate caption layers visible simultaneously on screen. Must SEE two distinct text elements at different positions (e.g., one at bottom, one in middle). NOT: consecutive captions where one replaces the other normally.
Sentence starts without a capital letter — only when you are certain it is the START of a new sentence, not a continuation of the previous line.

Wrong sentence splitting — flag when: (1) two separate sentences are merged into one caption, or (2) a caption breaks mid-phrase where a natural pause or clause boundary exists. Use transcript to identify sentence endings. Example: 'My monthly ThreadBeast box – just arrived you – take a quick style – quiz' breaks across three sentences incorrectly.

Caption split typo — A video editor accidentally left a duplicate word at a caption boundary. This only happens with SHORT, SPECIFIC, UNCOMMON words. Before you flag anything, check the word against the BLOCKED LIST below. If the word is on that list, STOP — do not flag, move on.

BLOCKED LIST (never flag these as split typos under any circumstances):
and, the, but, for, at, in, on, is, it, or, to, a, an, that, this, with, from, they, then, when, what, here, have, also, just, like, some, into, over, only, even, back, other, your, our, its, very, each, been, same, does, will, make, more, most, much, away, well, look, good, down, now, out, up, all, no, so, do, go, one, two, three, there, their, these, those, ingredients, product, formula, skin, face, body, hair, cream, serum, oil, water, use, using, really, actually, love, help, works, working, feel, feeling, know, think, want, need, see, get, got, new, first, last, every, always, never, after, before, because, which, where, about, still, more, less

An uncommon word that is NOT on the blocked list qualifies if: (1) it appears as the last word of caption N AND the first word of caption N+1, and (2) removing it from the START of caption N+1 still leaves a grammatically complete sentence. Only flag if both conditions are met.

CAUTION: for burned-in captions, OCR errors are common — a small word may be misread entirely. If you are not completely certain what the word is, do not flag.
Captions out of sync — footage clearly changes scenes or actions while the caption still shows the previous line, or captions appear before the matching words are spoken.
Typo in caption text — a word on screen is misspelled. Cross-reference against transcript to confirm the correct word. Flag the exact wrong word, timestamp, and what it should say.
Captions outside safe area — caption text is cut off at frame edges or positioned so close to the bottom or sides that it would be cropped on standard displays.

Missing disclaimer — if the video makes health claims, financial claims, results claims, or promotes a regulated product or service, check whether a disclaimer or legal text appears on screen at any point. Flag only when the content clearly warrants a disclaimer and none is present.

Incorrect pronunciation — review the transcript for words the talent appears to have mispronounced. A mispronunciation often shows in the transcript as an unexpected phonetic substitute. Only flag when you can confirm from context (brand name, medical term, product name) that the transcribed word differs from the intended word. Flag timestamp, word as heard, and what it should be. Do NOT flag if the difference could be a Whisper misread of a correctly pronounced specialized term.
do_not_flag: Lowercase mid-sentence continuations — only flag capitals at the start of new sentences
Specialized vocabulary mismatches driven by Whisper alone — trust the caption for ingredient, brand, botanical, and medical names unless the caption itself is visually wrong
'ingredients' at a caption boundary — this word is on the blocked list and must never be flagged as a split typo regardless of context

'and' at a caption boundary — this word is on the blocked list and must never be flagged as a split typo. 'and' is a natural sentence connector. Even if it appears at the end of one caption and the start of the next, do not flag
Any common English word appearing at a caption boundary — if the word is in normal everyday use, it belongs on the blocked list and should not be flagged
Any issue you cannot visually confirm from the frames or transcript — do not flag based on assumption
